RaceName: Supporting Ayr Racecourse, The John Cocking Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 50,000gns yearling; seventh foal; half-brother to winners Lauderdale (French/KSA/Qatari 1m-10.5f including 2yo; RPR 102), Firelight (6f 2yo; 100) and Antiphon (5f-6f; 87); dam 1m4f winner (AW/Listed; 107), sister to 12.5f Listed winner Oh Beautiful; good market support will bring him into the equation.

RaceName: Supporting Ayr Racecourse, The John Cocking Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-brother to four winners, two of them placed in Group races; beaten nearly 8l but still displayed significant promise when third of five in Newmarket novice (7f, good to firm) won by a good prospect last month; needs to be taken very seriously.

RaceName: Supporting Ayr Racecourse, The John Cocking Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Closely related to a fairly useful 6f 2yo winner; beaten about 11l when keeping-on third of six on last month's debut at Musselburgh (7f, good), showing only minor promise, but ought to have learnt from that experience.

RaceName: Supporting Ayr Racecourse, The John Cocking Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 68,000euros yearling; first foal; dam once-raced half-sister to winners Stormy Antarctic (1m/1m1f Group 2) and Al Johrah (Group-placed 5f 2yo), out of Listed-placed 1m/1m2f winner; with a good stable; no surprise if he features on debut.

RaceName: Supporting Ayr Racecourse, The John Cocking Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Cost 145,000gns as a yearling and is out of a fairly useful 7f AW winner; shaped well before fading into third of seven on debut at Beverley (7.4f, good) four weeks ago and will be a tough nut to crack if able to build on that performance here.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Now 3lb lower than when winning on AW at Newcastle (5f) in January; fair form on turf last twice, latest when 2l fourth at Thirsk (5f, good); has won on fast ground; each-way chance.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner
Spotlight: Won off an 11lb mark at Musselburgh last August (5f, good); hasn't run as well since and below-par over same C&D last time (good); dropped another 3lb so no surprise if he were to go well.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: All six wins, three on AW, have been over 6f; best run this season was when second over 5f at Ffos Las last month (good); chance on that run but this faster going not sure to suit.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Three of her four wins last season (5f/6f) were on fast ground, so conditions suit here; creditable second over C&D last week (good); a leading chance.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to win on turf, but is now 1lb lower than when winning at Newcastle in February (5f); could go well, although was beaten 10l here last time (6f, soft).

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Back to form in first-time cheekpieces when winning a selling handicap at Hamilton last time (5f, good to firm); only 2lb higher so not out of it with headgear on again.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Hamilton off 3lb lower in May (5f, good); close third over same C&D next time; each-way chance.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark in a maiden here in May (6f, soft; 200-1); not so good back in handicap company at Carlisle last time (6f, good); others stronger.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Best at 5f, but all his wins have come on AW or good/soft ground on turf; fair third on fast ground at Catterick two runs back (5f) but well beaten after being slowly away and losing a shoe over C&D last week.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Irish; five-race maiden; well beaten over 6f on both starts for Mike Smith; best watched for now.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Only turf win came off a 7lb lower mark at Musselburgh in June (5f, good); two decent runs since then, goes well on fast ground and one to consider.

RaceName: Racing To School Reaches 250,000 Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Probably fortunate when left clear to score at Stratford (2m3f, good to firm) last month but readily followed up in four-runner C&D (good) contest ten days ago and is evidently in prime form.

RaceName: Racing To School Reaches 250,000 Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Made all in mares' maiden hurdle here (2m1f, good) last summer and came second on her next three appearances; absent since October but her 12lb weight-for-age allowance will be handy on this chasing debut.

RaceName: Racing To School Reaches 250,000 Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Maiden who is not living up to her pedigree but seemed to enjoy front-running role and ran best race yet when clear second of ten in Worcester handicap chase (2m, good) 12 days ago; must be considered after that effort.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
OwnerName: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde
Spotlight: Three wins last June/July, the third over 5f here (good to soft); just as effective over 6f on fast ground and AW; neck second at Hamilton (5f, good) on Saturday and one to consider.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Has won four times, including twice here (5f/6f), but ideally suited by some give in the ground; proving hard to win with this season, although has run creditably on last four starts (on good to firm on first occasion); each-way chance.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
OwnerName: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing
Spotlight: 12-time winner, latest on AW in March (6f); fair running-on third over 5f here last time (good; Woohoo, who runs in the 2.30, was second); contender back up in trip.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark in a C&D handicap last week (good); has stiffer task with his penalty and was withdrawn on account of fast ground last month, but worth considering.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-George Scott; eight-race maiden and yet to hit form for Mike Smith (well beaten over 7f here last week).

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: All three wins have been over 5f on good ground, latest at Musselburgh last August; hasn't been in top form this term, best run when third at Musselburgh in June (5f); bit to prove, including his stamina.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Only win in 19 turf runs was at Hamilton in June (6f, good to soft); close third to Jm Jhingree (runs in the 2.30) in a 5f Hamilton seller last month (good) and close fourth in a 5f handicap there on Saturday; each-way chance.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed; well beaten on both starts in handicaps; best watched for now.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Useful on the Flat at his best and made the frame in two maiden hurdles in April 2025 (both 2m, good); this is his first run since and he doesn't set a daunting form standard but, nevertheless, he's a big player.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Bought for £4,000 after finishing third of nine in good-ground Irish point on debut in April; not an easy one to weigh up but has joined a top stable for rules career; tongue-tie on; market helpful.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Out of an unraced sister to high-class chaser Nacarat; made low-key rules debut when well-held fourth at Bangor (2m3f, good) last month but was placed in an Irish point in May and is open to improvement.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Soundly beaten in both bumpers last year but her fourth of six over hurdles at Fontwell (2m3f, good) last month was more encouraging and further progress is possible.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner
Spotlight: Dual turf winner in 2024 (6f/7.5f); has also won twice on the AW, most recently last December (7f), but rather out of form since and others are stronger.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Irish; 0-11; well beaten on stable debut at Carlisle nine days ago (1m1f, good) after nearly a year off but better can be expected today.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
OwnerName: David Orr & Miss L A Perratt
Spotlight: C&D winner in May (good to firm) and decent third at Hamilton later in the month (8.5f, good to firm); disappointing in two runs since, first-time cheekpieces latest (retained); could go well back on good to firm ground.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: Won maiden claimer for Tony Mullins in August 2024 (1m, good); hasn't won since but a few fair runs for new yard, including when third at Carlisle in June (1m1f, soft); blinkers now tried; fast ground is a concern.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins over 1m at Ripon last season (good/good to firm); a bit disappointing this term (4l fourth of seven at Pontefract latest) but on a good mark now.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: Only win in 23 runs was a 1m nursery at York in October 2023 for Ralph Beckett; badly out of form this season and cheekpieces now tried.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins (7f/1m) in August 2024; best run last season was when second over C&D last May (good to firm); could bounce back from a below-par reappearance run last month.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
OwnerName: N J Dalgarno & W R Ellis
Spotlight: 27-race maiden; creditable third in a C&D classified event eight days ago (good); this is tougher.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran who has won three of his 58 races on turf, the latest over this trip at Musselburgh last September (good); has been a bit disappointing this season.

RaceName: Racing Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Well treated on peak form but has struggled since returning from wind surgery and finished a well-beaten last of three over 3m2f here in May when he may not have stayed; this much shorter trip and first-time blinkers may help but he arrives with plenty to prove.

RaceName: Racing Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Completed a spring double last year off 8lb higher; struggled for form after returning in December from a break but fared better when back to front-running tactics at Stratford (extended 2m2f; good to firm) a fortnight ago and a repeat of that effort would put him in the mix.

RaceName: Racing Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Triple hurdle winner who has dropped to an attractive mark but for good reason; tailed off over further here last month and, although this shorter trip is a plus, she has plenty to prove.

RaceName: Racing Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Two creditable efforts since returning from a short break, latest when a close fourth over 2m1f here last month off this mark; stays this far and enters calculations.

RaceName: Racing Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 18-race maiden; twice a runner-up over C&D last summer; failed to hit those heights since but shaped with more encouragement on last month's return over C&D, as though the run was needed, and remains on a competitive mark if able to build on that effort.

RaceName: Racing Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Still chasing a first British success since his French 2023 hurdle win and, while he has posted a few placed efforts, he has failed to quite see out his races over trips beyond 2m; another 3lb lower but needs to raise his game.

RaceName: Racing Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Off the mark at the 25th attempt with a comfortable C&D success ten days ago; a 3lb rise looks fair and a repeat would make him a major player, though his overall profile remains patchy.

RaceName: Racing Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Returned from a break to register a third career success here (2m1f, good) last month, under Eleanor Williams; remains competitively treated with 3lb rise and capable of another bold showing if reproducing that performance.

RaceName: Racing Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual Flat winner (1m2f/1m4f) who had shown little over hurdles until stepped up in trip when a near 6l third behind Saucats over C&D ten days ago; meets that rival on 4lb better terms and enters calculations if building on that performance.

RaceName: Racing Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Kept on for third on handicap hurdle debut at Ffos Las (2m, good) in May but may not have stayed 2m4f when below that form last month; tongue-tie is fitted and this drop back in trip could see him in a better light.

RaceName: Racing Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has struggled outside bumpers; pulled up twice before trailing home last of eight most recently; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Racing Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Best effort when switched to a left-handed track for a 2m5f handicap at Fontwell in May last time and, although this drop back in trip is not an obvious plus, he remains unexposed and open to improvement.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Has modest 1-22 strike-rate and is better known as a sprinter but kept on for respectable fifth at Thirsk (7f, good to firm) recently; drops in grade here and is well worth another crack at this trip; interesting.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Gained five of his nine wins over C&D, the latest eight days ago (good ground); still on workable mark under 4lb penalty and ought to be right in the thick of things again.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: 2-4 since returned to turf in April, the latter win gained at Catterick (7f, good for firm) three weeks ago; effectively 6lb higher here if Conor Whiteley's reduced claim is considered but ought to go well again.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Safely held on consecutive 6f starts this spring but placed twice since returned to 7f, latterly in C&D (good) contest won by Jkr Cobbler three weeks ago; 2lb higher today but probably won't be far away.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to strike form after four runs for new stable this year (1m-1m2f) and her only win was gained over 1m2f on heavy ground; continues to slip down the weights but drops back to 7f with quite a bit to prove.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Just 1-22 but scores good marks for her consistency since 6f AW win in January; denied clear run before keeping on for fifth in recent C&D contest won by Jkr Cobbler; each-way claims at least.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: In the frame three consecutive times in recent weeks, most recently when fourth behind Jkr Cobbler over C&D (good) eight days ago, but remains without a win since spring 2024 and recent 2lb rise is not helpful.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Belied 33-1 odds when fourth at Musselburgh (7f, good) three weeks ago and is now 2lb lower than for last summer's win at Redcar (7f, good to soft) but her overall profile remains unconvincing.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Well handicapped on some of last year's form for Gemma Tutty but left that stable after some disappointing runs in the autumn and there has been no upturn after three starts for new trainer this year.

RaceName: Par Inn Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Made it 2-4 for this stable when winning a 2m1f handicap hurdle here in May and followed that with a respectable second off 5lb higher back here a fortnight later; has plenty in his favour on this chase debut.

RaceName: Par Inn Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Only one win from 18 runs over fences but he tends to run his race and has plenty of placed efforts to his name; below par when third off this mark over C&D last month and now wears a refitted tongue-tie; could quickly return to form.

RaceName: Par Inn Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Dual chase winner who has been more miss than hit since scoring in May last year; recent hurdle run in a first-time visor (retained) offered little encouragement, but he returns to fences off a handy mark; needs to rediscover his spark.

RaceName: Par Inn Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 13-race maiden, including three runs over fences, but showed more when travelling well for a long way last time, failing to stay 3m2f; needs to build on that and the drop back in trip is a plus; usually claimer ridden but not today.

RaceName: Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Came good for Jim Goldie at third attempt, in refitted cheekpieces at Hamilton last month, and bettered that form when close third in competitive race at York (also 1m1f, good) soon afterwards; not quite at same level over the extended 1m2f at York last time but still a major player here.

RaceName: Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to add to his two C&D wins in spring 2024 but got back in the groove with two placed runs last month, the latter in Class 4 C&D contest; ought to pose a potent threat at this lower level.

RaceName: Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: 0-14 overall but has performed respectably for new stable this year, most recently when fourth at Carlisle (1m3f, good to soft) last month; continues to edge down the weights and ought to be suited by the reduced emphasis on stamina here; not out of this.

RaceName: Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Low-mileage filly who kept on for clear second behind unexposed rival on last month's handicap debut at Hamilton (8.3f, good); moves back up in trip and competes off same mark here; commands respect.

RaceName: Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Safely held over 7f recently and now 0-7 but made the frame on consecutive 8.3f starts at Nottingham in April, and the dam's side of her pedigree suggests she's well worth a crack at this new trip.

RaceName: William Hill Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Good-ground specialist with nine chase wins to his name, latest off 2lb higher in July last year; solid front-running third at Bangor (2m4f, good) 17 days ago after a break and he holds leading claims.

RaceName: William Hill Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Irish hunter chase winner who has gone very close on both starts for this stable, beaten a neck at Fontwell (3m2f, good) and by a short head at Market Rasen (2m7f, good) nine days later; up in class but looks to have a strong chance of doubling his chase tally.

RaceName: William Hill Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed the benefit of a fourth wind operation when winning over hurdles at Fontwell (2m3f, good) in May and only narrowly denied by an in-form Skelton horse off 3lb higher two weeks later; below par since but he returns to fences on a competitive mark.

RaceName: William Hill Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Multiple hurdle/chase winner but he's lost his way since a 2m4f success at Ffos Las (good to soft) last June and has offered little in three runs this season; well treated on his best form but with plenty to prove.

RaceName: William Hill Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Encouraging first two efforts for this stable before a lesser run prompted wind surgery; got back on track to some extent at Bangor (3m, good) last month and, while more is needed, he's likely to have a say.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Placed over hurdles this season and was third (albeit no match for wide-margin winner) in Cumberland Plate at Carlisle (1m3f, good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) when back on the Flat for new stable last month; respected off same mark in this easier race.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Made winning handicap debut for Ed Walker at Ffos Las (1m4f, soft) last summer but was sold for 13,000gns after a lesser effort in September and this stable debut comes after a 292-day break; market may guide.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Took step back in right direction when second of five at Musselburgh (2m, good) last month but was unable to lay a glove on Cascade Hall and will need to build on that performance here.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins this spring, latterly when making all to score by over 6l at Musselburgh (2m, good) last month; safely held when making the frame on next two outings (2m/2m1f) but still enters calculations.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Gained three of his nine wins over this C&D and gave good account when staying-on third over C&D (good) a week ago; still on the same workable mark and has another big role to play.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Long in the tooth now and needs to better his recent efforts but was close third off 4lb higher at Hamilton (1m1f, good to firm) in May and is probably better suited by today's trip; can't rule out.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: 120,000gns yearling; looked in trouble at the halfway stage at Nottingham (6f, good) in May but she finished well to snatch an unlikely victory at 12-1; found things tough in the Albany at Royal Ascot but she's back in calmer waters here and is open to progress on this step up to 7f; not ruled out on nursery debut.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Nursery Handicap
OwnerName: Sarah Fern, Patricia James And Partner
Spotlight: Left his debut form well behind when runner-up at Leicester (7f, good) last month and he took another step forward when scoring at Newcastle (7f, AW) 18 days ago; rallied well to beat a clear second in that race and he's open to more progress now switched to a nursery; respected.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Finished down the field in his first two runs but he found improvement when runner-up in a Windsor maiden (6f, good to firm) in May; needs to build on that but he should have more to offer and his dam was a 7f winner as a 2yo.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Did best of the newcomers when an encouraging third on her Goodwood debut (6f, soft) last month; 5-2 for a fast-ground Newbury novice 11 days later and she went down fighting in a close second of ten; looks on a fair mark now switched to a nursery and she's an interesting contender at this new trip.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Showed promise in three novice events, most recently when runner-up over an extended 7f at Beverley (good to firm) in May; should have a future in handicaps but opening mark is no gift and she needs to find more after a short break.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Justified support when making a winning C&D debut (good to soft) in June, keeping on well despite showing signs of inexperience; more on his plate under a penalty but he's entitled to improve; should be thereabouts.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £5,000 purchase as a 3yo; half-brother to winners Lofgren (useful 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase) and Gatacre Street (2m7f chase); newcomer from a respected stable and worth monitoring in the market.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £27,000 purchase after winning her sole Irish point in May and made the perfect start for her new stable when landing a C&D bumper last month (first-time hood; retained); had plenty in hand and she's a leading contender under her penalty.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well-related 4yo who was sent off 8-1 for his debut at Fontwell in May but finished a well-beaten seventh of nine; likely to have benefited from the run though much more is needed.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Second foal; out of an unraced dam closely related to winners Urbanist (2m-3m hurdle/chase) and Lilting Verse (bumper/2m5f hurdle/2m7f chase), likely to benefit from this debut and looks one for the longer term.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Hinted at ability in two bumpers last season, most recently when staying on into fifth at Chepstow in April; likely to be seen in a better light over hurdles, particularly when tackling longer trips.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: J L Lightfoot- D V Gardner
Spotlight: Half-sister to winners Bagheera Ginge (2m2f-3m2f hurdle/chase) and Champ Is Real (3m chase); makes debut and bred to have a future but likely one for further down the line.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed more when a 9l sixth of seven at Stratford (2m, good to firm) on second start, though the form is open to question in a steadily run race; improvement is still required.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Seventh foal; half-sister to useful winners Perfect Attitude (point/2m hurdle), Dragon Bones (bumper/2m4f-3m hurdle) and Flawless Escape (bumper/2m4f hurdle); pedigree offers encouragement but only of interest if attracting support.

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who made all for 2l success in five-runner maiden at Goodwood (5f, good) most recently; looks all about speed and warrants respect in another sharp scenario with further improvement plausible.

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Close third of seven in Nottingham nursery (5f; first-time visor, now discarded) last time; the most exposed contender in this field, as a record of 1-8 reflects (gained the win in seller); however, he's the solid angle, being a consistent sort.

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: 360,000euros breeze-up purchase who has substance to go with her promise; finished third to smart rival (subsequent Group 2 scorer) on debut and the form of her Lingfield success (5f, good to firm) has received a few boosts; looks likely to do well in nurseries.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Dubawi colt; half-brother to winners Saxon Warrior (1m inc 2yo/2,000 Guineas; RPR 122), Drumroll (1m/1m2f inc Group 3; 110), More Beautiful (Listed-placed 5f 2yo; 102) and Pavlenko (7f; 91); dam 6f/7f 2yo winner (inc Group 1; 114); top pedigree but Lordan prefers stablemate.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Mehmas colt; E260,000 yearling; half-brother to winners Bear Story (1m-10.6f AW inc 2yo/Group 3/Australia; RPR 110) and Slieve Bearnagh (7f-1m2f Flat, 71; 2m/2m1f hurdle); dam 1m2f winner (96), half-sister to HK/Singapore 1m-1m2f Group 1 winner Dunboyne Express; respected source and lots of appeal on paper.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Frankel colt; dam 6f-8.5f winner (inc 2yo/US Grade 1; RPR 110), sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Sacred Bridge, closely related to 1m2f Listed winner Crossed Baton, out of 9.4f/1m2f winner from fine family; attractive pedigree and yard in red-hot form.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Churchill colt; E40,000 yearling; half-brother to winners Spirit Of Saxony (7f AW; RPR 89), Coolmeen Vega (Spanish 7.5f-8.5f) and Pure Circle (Italian 6f); dam unraced half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Jessica's Dream (dam of 1m Group 1 winner Havana Gold); looks yard's second string on rider arrangements.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Regally-bred Wootton Bassett colt was sent off 6-1 on debut at the Curragh 16 days ago and found just stablemate Giant Sequoia too good; pulled nicely clear of the third that day and likely to improve plenty for that initial experience; preferred by Lordan; the one to beat.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Zarak colt; E65,000 yearling; bought for 75,000gns at the Breeze Ups in April; unraced dam closely related to Australian 1m2f Listed winner Astronomos, half-sister to Gold Cup winner Courage Mon Ami; preferred by Browne-McMonagle.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Has a patchy record but he turned things around with an 5l win over 1m6f at Southwell (AW; 7-4 favourite) last Sunday; looks well treated under a penalty for that success and his penultimate win was at this track; strong contender.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: In and out since winning at Leicester (1m4f, good) in April and he was beaten 8l after a slow start at Newbury last time; now only 1lb higher than for that last success but he needs to raise his game back up in trip.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: All four wins have been at Wolverhampton (1m4f-2m) and latest was 11 months ago; finished a close third off 1lb higher at Bath (1m6f, good) last month but he's not easy to predict and was well held with cheekpieces tried at Chester last time; the headgear is quickly discarded but he comes with risks attached.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Made the breakthrough at the 12th attempt when justifying support at Ffos Las (1m4f, good to soft) last month; 4lb higher in different conditions here but he pulled well clear with the runner-up last time and his best form has come on Polytrack; respected back up in trip.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: His last win was 14 months ago but he made some good late headway when runner-up at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm) last month; only 1lb higher here and he looks interesting back up in trip.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Six-time winner, with four at Windsor (11.4f) and two on Polytrack (1m2f/2m); her last success was off 2lb higher last autumn but she's been disappointing in last two runs and needs to get back on track.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Her first four wins were on turf but she made the breakthrough on AW when finishing well to land an apprentice handicap at Kempton (1m3f) last Wednesday; now steps back up in trip but she beat an odds-on rival last time and escapes a penalty for that success; dangerous.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Well treated on his old form for the Johnstons but he returned after a mammoth absence last month and has been tailed off at big prices in both runs (1m4f/2m) for current yard; lots to prove.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Dual turf winner at 1m2f and 1m3f for Charlie Johnston last spring; has had some struggles since but he got back to form with a bang when winning by 7l here (1m4f, AW) last Monday; that was off a tumbling mark and he's a major player if he can repeat that form under a penalty.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Nine of his ten wins have come on AW and he's a strong stayer at up to 2m; his last win was off this mark at Kempton in February but he flopped at that track last month and needs to bounce back.

RaceName: Fitzdares Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: J Collins, P Moorhead, C & J Cooley
Spotlight: Showed a plucky attitude to nose ahead in the final strides at Nottingham (1m, good) and build on his debut promise; open to further improvement and warrants respect even with a 5lb penalty.

RaceName: Fitzdares Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Seems one for handicaps on the bare form of his two runs; however, may show improvement back down in trip, having seemed a non-stayer over 1m2f at Windsor latest.

RaceName: Fitzdares Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 45,000euros foal, 135,000gns yearling; Teofilo half-brother to three winners, notably Alashar (Listed-placed 7f/9.5f; RPR 101); dam 6f-1m winner (including Group 3; 112); wears tongue-tie on debut; market instructive.

RaceName: Fitzdares Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Recorded only a modest RPR over C&D last time and needs to take another step forward; may be more interesting in handicaps shortly.

RaceName: Fitzdares Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Too Darn Hot gelding who showed abundant promise with fast-finishing third of 12 under 3lb claimer in 1m AW maiden at Southwell; the form has received a couple of boosts and he is open to progress; major player.

RaceName: Fitzdares Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Placed in sole German start; less solid on his two British efforts and may find better opportunities in handicaps soon.

RaceName: Fitzdares Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Gestuet Schlenderhan Gmbh U Co Kg
Spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who has consistent 1m/1m2f form and managed to run creditably despite traffic issues at Salisbury most recently, albeit couldn't finish the job once finding a gap; has solid place claims back over 1m.

RaceName: Fitzdares Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Always behind in C&D contest having been friendless in the betting; bred to need longer trips.

RaceName: Fitzdares Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has something to find on her 7f efforts and therefore needs improvement for this extra furlong; handicaps may be her ideal scene.

RaceName: Fitzdares Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Registered only a meagre RPR in 7f AW maiden at Wolverhampton and is probably one for handicaps later on.

RaceName: Fitzdares Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £72,000 yearling; Dark Angel sister to three winners, notably Diabolic (US 5f-6.5f turf/dirt 2yo including Listed); dam Group-placed 5f 2yo winner (97); judged solely on breeding, this newcomer has a doubt over the 1m trip.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Race
Spotlight: AW maiden winner and Listed second for Charlie Appleby last year; didn't cut much ice in three starts at Meydan for Bhupat Seemar and now makes debut for Andy Slattery; yard looks to have stronger claims with Loughrea, so likely best watched.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Race
Spotlight: Disappointed when a beaten favourite on sole start at two but much better form this year; close second over this C&D (good) on return in May; justified short odds to win an ordinary maiden at Limerick by 6l on latest (1m, good); rated 89 so a fair bit to find with Straight Up, but going the right way and his yard remains in terrific form.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Race
OwnerName: Flaxman Stables, Mrs Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith
Spotlight: Won on debut at Listowel last September (7f, heavy); two of three subsequent starts have come in Group 3s over 1m at Newmarket (sixth of 7 beaten 3.75l) and on latest in first-time hood (retained) over 1m2f at Leopardstown (last of 5 beaten 5.5l); rated 99, so obvious chance on the figures in this lesser race.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Ran into some trouble before keeping on well when fourth on Yarmouth debut (6f, good) in May; never-dangerous sixth in the Woodcote at Epsom last month but that was a respectable effort from a wide draw and he's open to progress on this step up in trip; in the mix for in-form yard.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: £25,000 breeze-up 2yo; 100-30 favourite on Wolverhampton debut (6f, AW) last month but he ran green before finishing a well-held seventh of ten; should know more this time but he needs a transformation upped in trip.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 15,000euros foal, 27,000gns yearling; third foal; half-brother to Italian winners Sayidah Poets (7.5f/1m 2yo) and Siguanaba (6f-1m including 2yo); dam maiden (third 10.5f in France) sister to useful 7f/1m winner Brave Hero, half-sister to US 1m stakes winner Double Touch; yard 1-7 with 2yos this year and market should guide on debut.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 19,000euros foal, 24,000euros yearling; half-brother to 1m/1m3f winner Lennox (including AW/Australia; RPR 83); dam 13.3f AW winner (82), half-sister to winners James Garfield (6f 2yo Group 2) and Hallelujah U (useful 1m 2yo/1m4f); drawn out wide on debut and he could be one for later on.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Gelded son of Sea The Moon; 20-1 and he ran green throughout when a well-held fourth at Kempton (7f, AW) two weeks ago; should know more this time but he needs a major step forward on his second start.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Half-brother to winners Rydale Frosty (6f 2yo; RPR 94), City Of York (1m/1m1f including AW; 87), Thorntonledale Max (7f AW; 76) and Dalby Forest (5f AW; 71); dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo/AW; 104), out of multiple 5f winner; only the third 2yo runner for yard this year but he needs watching in market.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 8,000euros yearling; out of a sister to a 7f Group 3 juvenile winner in Germany but he made a low-key start when sixth of eight here (7f, good to firm) in May; should be more streetwise this time but he needs to leave that debut form well behind.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Tailed off at big prices in his three runs and latest was over this trip at Kempton (AW); hood is now added but he can only be watched.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Out of an unraced half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Dandhu; showed only minor promise when eighth of 13 at Yarmouth (6f, good to firm) last month and others are preferred.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 36,000gns foal, 27,000gns yearling; second foal; dam unraced half-sister to useful winners Tannaaf (7f 2yo/1m2f), Danjeu (1m4f) and Waheebah (10.5f-1m6f), out of 1m4f winner; yard 4-10 with 2yos this season and she needs a close look on debut.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Half-sister to French winners Sandon (1m2f 2yo) and Betty Nelson (10.5f); made a promising start when a clear third at Salisbury (6f, soft) and she built on that with a near-miss over 7f at Newcastle (AW) 18 days ago; sets the standard on that form and she's open to more progress; strong contender.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: RPR of 46 when well held in 6f events at Goodwood (soft) and Newbury (good to firm); has a lot to find upped in trip and nurseries will be more suitable.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 18,000gns yearling; Ardad half-brother to four winners, notably Astronomy's Choice (7f 2yo; RPR 96); dam unplaced 1m (57); wears hood on debut; yard is 1-6 with 2yos this term.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Both starts over C&D, better effort when running-on fifth of 11 latest; difficult to rule out another step forward but he looks the type who'll be more interesting in nurseries shortly.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: May benefit from the faster surface but needs to improve plenty on his Salisbury soft-ground effort.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 3,500euros yearling, 16,000euros breeze-up 2yo; by Space Traveller and first foal of 7f/1m winner (RPR 79); one of several newcomers in the field; market instructive.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Showed promise with fourth of ten in Newbury event (6f, good to firm) that saw the only runners with experience filled the first two places; open to progress and is one of the main contenders.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 72,000gns foal, 260,000gns yearling; Perfect Power half-brother to three winners, notably Chalk Mountain (multiple 7f AW; RPR 91) and Malvern (6f 2yo; 79); dam 5f winner (95); notable connections; respected debutant.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Due Diligence gelded half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Q T Pie (RPR 67), out of 1m2f winner (72); sibling gained the success over C&D, albeit second time out; check the betting.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Showed some promise with sixth of 11 at Salisbury (6f, soft) despite proving green, losing a shoe and perhaps even finding the ground softer than ideal; interesting with improvement on the cards.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Came from further back than the first two in 6f AW maiden at Kempton, finishing third of ten; helps to set the form standard and there is a chance he can show progress to boot.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Has plenty to find on Goodwood soft-ground debut and is a similar type to Goodwood Meteor in that she may improve for this quicker surface.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Has weak claims on form and is likely to be more at home in modest nurseries shortly.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 100,000gns foal, 160,000euros yearling; by Mehmas and first foal of 6f/7f winner (including Group 3; RPR 113); stable's record of 2-8 with 2yos in 2026 includes a successful newcomer this month; interesting.

RaceName: SIS Supporting Irish Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Won a Bellewstown maiden last year (1m, good); okay return in the Madrid at Naas (7f, soft) in March; not as good upped to 1m1f on latest at Leopardstown (soft); drops back in trip now and visor goes on; could rebound on this sounder surface.

RaceName: SIS Supporting Irish Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Enjoying a good season with wins in handicap at Cork (1m, soft) in May and a rated race at Leopardstown (1m, good) last month; beat Glitter And Glory by half a length the last day and 5lb worse off here, so may not uphold that form.

RaceName: SIS Supporting Irish Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Dundalk maiden winner; 0-2 on turf; over 4.25l behind Johnny Soda (and Glitter And Glory) in rated race at Leopardstown on latest; 7lb swing could enable him to close that gap on his handicap debut.

RaceName: SIS Supporting Irish Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Consistent filly rarely runs a bad race; won a maiden at Dundalk in March; not beaten far in Curragh handicap in May; 0.5l behind Johnny Soda in rated race at Leopardstown latest and weighted to turn that around now; big chance.

RaceName: SIS Supporting Irish Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Definite promise in maidens on the AW over 7f-1m and on latest at Naas (1m, good); could be on a decent mark for her first handicap and worth checking in the market.

RaceName: SIS Supporting Irish Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Didn't show a whole lot in maidens at 7f-9.5f on varying ground; gelded after mid-field finish at Gowran in May and now makes handicap debut; yard also runs Vishaya; the market can guide.

RaceName: SIS Supporting Irish Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Won off 13lb lower at Gowran (7f, soft) in April; let backers down when beaten favourite at Leopardstown (7f, sft-hvy) but bounced back last time when a good second at Cork (1m, good); raised 2lb for that and this is a deeper race, so more needed.

RaceName: SIS Supporting Irish Racing Handicap
Spotlight: All three handicap runs on good ground have been fine efforts; runner up at Down Royal (7f), won at Fairyhouse (6f, good) and kept on for a 0.5l second back there over 7f on latest; nudged up another 3lb and this is harder, but the step up to 1m could eke out more improvement, so not discounted.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: 16-race maiden but he was runner-up three times last season and wasn't beaten far when fifth in new headgear at Kempton (6f, AW) 11 days ago; that was eye-catching from off the pace and he's in the mix back up in trip.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Seven-time AW winner and his last success was over 6f here last October; now 2lb lower than for that win but has a mixed record since and was never involved at Wolverhampton last Monday; comes with risks attached and he needs to raise his game back up in trip; cheekpieces reapplied.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: First and second in two C&D novices the winter before last but, having been off for a year, he's trailed home last in three handicaps in 2026; still unexposed but he has plenty to prove for new yard (left Amanda Perrett for 1,200gns).

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Turf and dual Tapeta winner who justified favouritism with a narrow success at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in March; still looks feasibly treated on that form but he flopped at Bath (1m, firm) last time and needs to bounce back after that heavy defeat.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Won 6f maiden at Navan as 2yo; promising stable debut over 6f on this track in February 2025 but he's mostly struggled since; now 27lb below his peak mark but he was laboured over C&D last time and others are preferred.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Recorded his 11th AW win when scoring at Wolverhampton (7f) in May; good second over C&D next time and he had a wide trip when a respectable fourth back at Wolverhampton 11 days ago; should get a good tow into this race and he's respected back on Polytrack.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: All wins at 6f on ground ranging from good to soft to heavy, the last three in 2024; well handicapped on his old form but he's been tailed off in three runs (1m, AW/good to firm) for new yard this year and has a lot to prove

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Triple turf winner who is 1-19 on AW; perked up with a close call at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in December but he's not come close to that form in three subsequent runs; may have needed his Nottingham reappearance last month but his tactical speed will be tested back at this trip and others are more convincing.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Looks interesting on some runner-up efforts (6f/7f) last summer but he was quiet during the winter including over 7f on Polytrack; record is now 0-12 and he has something to prove after 155 days off.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Two 1m wins on Polytrack this spring, in a Lingfield classified event and a Kempton handicap; finished runner-up three times since her last success including at Wolverhampton (7f) and Ffos Las (1m) in last two runs; up another 2lb but she still looks feasibly treated and has leading claims.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Can miss the break but he's on a dangerous mark and was runner-up here (7f, good) in May before a respectable fourth at Chepstow last month; this return to AW is no problem and he has each-way claims.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
OwnerName: G E Bassett & P R Chamings
Spotlight: Defied this mark at Salisbury (6f, heavy) last September but she's had only two runs since and has struggled in both of them; still unexposed on AW but she needs a major revival on her second run after break.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Four-time turf winner who is 0-10 on AW but he gave it a good shot from the front when fourth over C&D last month; 1lb lower now and has possibilities if he can build on that last run.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who has finished a well-held fifth in two 6f handicaps (AW/soft) but it's still early days and he's a possible improver at this new trip; one to keep an eye on.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Only eighth of 11 at Salisbury (6f) but seemed to need the run and looks open to improvement; that race has thrown up a few winners since.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Only eighth of 13 in C&D maiden won by useful sort but appeared to need the outing and may be capable of better.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Ended up last of ten in Newbury contest (6f) having pulled hard; chance of improvement largely depends on whether he settles better.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Related to several winners for his connections; never landed a blow in 5f event at Salisbury (finished sixth of nine) but may show improvement upped in distance with the experience under his belt.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
OwnerName: Mr And Mrs B V Sangster
Spotlight: May benefit from the return to turf but seems one for nurseries, having finished out of the money in both starts; debut form has admittedly received a few boosts.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 175,000euros foal, 450,000euros (breeze-up) 2yo; Blue Point half-brother to Croupier (7f/1m winner, including 2yo; RPR 108) and two pretty useful winners; dam unraced sister to 7f 2yo Group 2 winner Saamidd; holds a couple of major entries; notable connections; respected newcomer.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Twice-raced colt who ran better than the bare result (seventh of nine) suggests at Newbury (6.5f) last time, albeit unable to make his experience count in a race where all of his rivals were newcomers; the form pick in this field.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Mehmas colt; dam 6f AW 2yo winner (RPR 84), out of 7f-winning half-sister to useful winners Kehaar (7f-1m) and All For Laura (5f 2yo; dam of 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Misheer); in good hands; interesting debutant.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Only ninth of 12 in C&D maiden but is evidently thought capable of much better, having been backed into favouritism; one to bear in mind, especially if the money again comes for him.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Down the field in both outings and appears to be crying out for an imminent switch to nurseries.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Has weak claims on form and is likely to be more at home in modest nurseries shortly.

RaceName: Killarney Glamping Handicap
OwnerName: Just The Two Of Us Partnership
Spotlight: Latest of five wins was in the 2023 renewal of this event, after which he ran well in a Group 3 at Leopardstown and a Listed contest over C&D; not the same force nowadays and has come down a good bit in the ratings; was still capable of good form last year; not at his best on AW in the spring and back from a break here.

RaceName: Killarney Glamping Handicap
Spotlight: Often made the frame in France, but just a 1m maiden win to his name; picked up out of a claimer in August, a frequent source of recruits to this yard; almost always wore hood/tongue-tie combination; goes without any aids here; long absence and surprise if he's ready to be involved.

RaceName: Killarney Glamping Handicap
Spotlight: Three wins from eight AW starts, but 0-20 on turf; did well at Dundalk over the winter and carried that form through to soft-ground contests in the spring; second over 7f at Naas and fourth over 1m2f at Leopardstown in April (strong form); not so good lately; likes to come from off the pace.

RaceName: Killarney Glamping Handicap
Spotlight: Wonderfully durable handicapper with 11 wins to his name, the latest being on his favoured soft ground at Roscommon in September; returned to form at that track two starts back, where he handled good ground, and fine run at Limerick (yielding) last month when narrowly beaten by an in-form 3yo; easier ground would be ideal, but still the one to beat.

RaceName: Killarney Glamping Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden has finished second five times, twice at Ballinrobe and three times at Leopardstown; on second start of the season, was backed at latter venue when front-running second over 1m1f; that was on soft to heavy, but he's fine on good; doesn't have to lead, but mightn't be much competition for it here.

RaceName: Killarney Glamping Handicap
Spotlight: Had placed form over 6f/7f at three, but sole win came when making all over 1m7f in amateur riders' handicap at Leopardstown last summer; twice runner-up in maiden hurdles as well; this will be too sharp a test on first run since October.

RaceName: Killarney Glamping Handicap
Spotlight: Won in Poland at two and over 1m1f in France last year; picked up for 9,000 euros in November; 80-1, never a factor over 10.5f on Irish debut at Roscommon.

RaceName: Killarney Glamping Handicap
Spotlight: Five wins for Joe Murphy, latest of them over 1m1f at Tipperary in October; big prices and has shown little in four starts for this yard; mark tumbling, but prefers it soft and will be of more interest when the rain returns.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Flopped badly when odds-on at Wolverhampton (7f) in May and now has a point to prove but scored over C&D on handicap debut in March and could still have a lot more to offer if back on song.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up over 7f on all three 2yo starts but was last of ten when upped to 1m for last month's seasonal/handicap debut; tries sprinting with a tongue-tie added today; not an easy one to weigh up at the moment.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Fillies' Handicap
OwnerName: Mildmay Racing Pl & Only Fillies Rc
Spotlight: Seemed to benefit from combination of drop back in trip and new cheekpieces when scoring at Goodwood (6f, soft) last month; recent 5lb rise asks quite a lot of her but this return to Polytrack won't be a problem.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who kept on for fourth when dropped back to 6f for recent handicap debut at Kempton; needs to build on that performance here but has the potential if aided by new tongue-tie.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Six-race maiden who ended last season with a disappointing 7f run and made a low-key return over that trip last month; might have needed that recent outing but others in this line-up have much less to prove.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Scored off reduced mark at Yarmouth (6f, soft) last month and also ran well when third at Newmarket (5f, good) ten days later; suited by his C&D and can make her presence felt again.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who displayed improvement when close second in 5f novice here in December; makes handicap debut after seven-month absence but solid market support will bring her into the equation.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Showed similar level of fair form on all four 2yo starts, most recently when fourth on nursery debut at Chelmsford (7f) in December; might have more to offer back at 6f this year but has fitness to prove after seven-month break.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Made a successful 2yo debut but he was soon in deep water and his form deteriorated; seen only twice in 2025 but things have stabilised somewhat and he's run two good races over C&D (good to firm), including 16 days ago; not eye-wateringly well handicapped but shouldn't be far away.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Multiple winner but turf successes are thin on the ground now, just one in over three years; however, he's been running up to his best since back on grass, going down narrowly in a higher grade at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) in May and another solid effort there last time; thereabouts.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Majority of her turf wins have come at Bath and Chepstow, most recently when making all on the former track in June, but she's a C&D winner too (good to firm) and she lost her chance early when back on this course 16 days ago; on a career-high mark but not ruled out.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Made all/most to take two 6f races last August/September (on good and heavy) but this year has got off to a terrible start with crushing defeats at Newmarket and Chester; risky for now; yard also runs An Outlaw's Grace.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Hooded in three juvenile starts but not as 3yo, returning with a quite convincing win on handicap debut over C&D (good to firm) from one who won either side of that race; unable to improve on that at Newmarket next time when 7lb higher but she was tight for room at one stage and still has potential.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Unraced as 2yo but her Newbury debut was promising last term and she followed it with an Ayr maiden win (6f, good to firm); seen only twice more that year, ending with a low-key run in Listed event, and she was beaten a long way at Newmarket on her return from eight months off; however, she showed a glimmer of hope before dropping out and Tom Marquand has his first ride of 2026 for the yard (2-9 in the last five years); trainer won this in 2024.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Two sprint wins in Ireland as 2yo, including when making all at the Curragh (6f, good) last August, but  in poor form on turf and the AW in Britain this year and it's hard to believe he's worth his current mark.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has a record of 2-7 over C&D (both wins on good to firm) and although not in quite the same form this year as she was last summer, such as when going close off 13lb higher on this track, she wasn't far away back here two starts ago; no surprise to see her go well.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Losing turf run goes back over two years but his last three wins on grass have come over C&D, including on good to firm, and he ran another good race on this track two weeks ago; has better claims than many.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Win haul includes two C&D wins but he missed all of 2025, was beaten a long way on his return from an absence and then failed to finish at Newbury in June; has it all to prove with refitted blinkers added to regular hood.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 5f winner here; she was involved in tight finishes at Bath (extended 5f; good to soft and heavy) last October and over 5f here in May but more often than not she tends to run out of steam over this trip.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has run well over C&D in maiden and novice events but her win came over 5f on Southwell AW and she has yet to cut the mustard in handicaps; her mark is sliding slowly but she needs more for the win.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Promising start when runner up on debut at Limerick (1m4f, yielding); upset in the stalls before running moderately on soft to heavy at Cork (1m4f); could do better back on a sounder surface, but more needed.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Modest debut on AW last year, but much better efforts this season in maidens at Naas (10.5f, good) and back at Dundalk (1m4f) on latest; rated 74 so still a fair bit to find to win, but definite place chance.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Both runs on soft ground; kept on nicely enough on debut at Gowran (9.5f); another solid effort upped to 1m4f at Cork latest when she had Benaki over 5l behind; pedigree suggests she could take another step forward on this quicker ground.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: 28-1 but far from disgraced on debut at Down Royal (10.5f, soft); open to improvement over this slightly longer trip and dam acted on good ground, so should handle conditions.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Camelot filly; half-sister to 7f 2yo winner Promo Queen (RPR 72); dam 1m/1m2f AW winner (RPR 73), half-sister to winners Erhaaf (1m2f), Ruzeiz (French 1m3f) and Jasaara (1m2f), out of unraced sister to Arc winner Sakhee; yard in red-hot form; check market.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Huge odds and well beaten in both starts; seems safe to rule out.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Barnane Stud & Hollywood Stud Ireland Limited
Spotlight: Sent off at 10-1 on debut at Leopardstown (1m5f, gd-fm) and kept on well for a 5.25l fifth of 10; not sure this drop in trip will suit on that evidence, but yard continues in terrific form, so can't be ruled out.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: No impact at big odds in maidens at Naas and Fairyhouse; will qualify for handicaps after this.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Rated 77 after three starts at 1m-9.5f; got to within 4.5l of the smart Sparan Nua on second start at Gowran (9.5f, good); found just one too good when ridden positively in Down Royal maiden on latest (10.5f, soft); may be more to come at this longer trip and this track could suit; chance.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: 0-8 and rated 82; all starts over 1m this season; 1.75l third in Naas maiden on seasonal return on soft; no impact in two stakes races and a handicap subsequently, but back on song in first time blinkers (retained) returned to maiden company at Naas (1m, good); worth a go at this trip and should go well again.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: Went close at Wolverhampton (8.6f) and Chepstow (1m) before getting off the mark (13th attempt) in a classified event at Wolverhampton last Tuesday; pulled 5l clear with his main market rival in that race and he hit a personal best with that brave success; key player.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: Won a classified event over C&D in January but he's not hit the same level since and was out the back at Brighton (1m2f) last month; now drops back in trip but he's not easy to predict and overall record is 1-18; others are more convincing.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: 12-race maiden who has been struggling to get competitive this year and has been well held in both runs for current yard; down the list.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: Two respectable efforts on AW (1m2f/9.4f) this year but he struggled in a classified event here (1m4f) when last seen in March; gelded since but he's now 0-9 and needs a major revival back in trip after a break.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: 0-13 but she went close at Nottingham (8.3f, good) in April and wasn't beaten far after missing the break in a Windsor handicap (1m, good to firm) last month; has claims if things go more to plan this time and she's respected back on AW.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: In-form 4yo who has been placed at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in his last two starts including when runner-up in a handicap last Monday; usually comes from off the pace and has claims if he gets the breaks.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: Dual AW winner who was placed on turf here (1m2f) last month but he flopped on his drop back to 1m at Bath (started slowly) 12 days ago; needs to bounce back after that and he's probably more potent over longer trips these days.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: Both wins have come over C&D but latest was in January 2025; finished third over 7f here in March but he's struggled in both subsequent runs and others are preferred.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: Disappointing favourite on debut last May and she's finished tailed off again in both subsequent runs; now drops into a 0-50 event but she needs a transformation back on AW.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: Beaten 14l or more in three novice events (6f/7f) last season, with a best RPR of 29; now switches to a classified event but she needs lots of improvement upped to 1m on her return.

RaceName: Find Us At fitzdares.com Handicap
Spotlight: Successful for two previous yards and ran creditably at Ayr on debut for current stable; below par in two starts since but likely found 6f too sharp latest; returns to a more suitable distance.

RaceName: Find Us At fitzdares.com Handicap
Spotlight: Prevailed by a neck at Sandown (1m, good) last time, registering a first success on turf; back up 3lb but remains well treated on the pick of his AW form; gained his other wins back-to-back.

RaceName: Find Us At fitzdares.com Handicap
Spotlight: Made a successful AW debut on final start for previous yard but has failed to back up the form kept to that sphere for new connections; returns from a layoff and reverts to turf; follow the market signals.

RaceName: Find Us At fitzdares.com Handicap
Spotlight: Has a better strike-rate on AW than turf and remains on a career-high mark, so seems up against it; however, consistent record (12233) at Windsor includes a good effort (neck second) in this contest last year; solid contender back here.

RaceName: Find Us At fitzdares.com Handicap
Spotlight: Seems best when fresh and duly ran well (close second) at Bath on seasonal debut; appeared to be a disappointing favourite at Epsom later in April but that effort is excusable with the race likely coming sooner than ideal; only attempt under Tom Marquand resulted in sole win (off current mark to boot); respected on return from 11-week break.

RaceName: Find Us At fitzdares.com Handicap
Spotlight: Novice winner for previous yard; 0-6 for current stable but ran encouragingly, despite being left with plenty to do, in last two starts; caught the attention of the stewards at Ffos Las (1m) latest; interesting with Oisin Murphy up for first time, assuming the cards drop right.

RaceName: Find Us At fitzdares.com Handicap
Spotlight: Steadily progressive colt who has form figures of 21423121 (turf/AW) since handicapping; comfortably justified favouritism dropped back to 1m at Southwell eight days ago; 1-1 at Windsor (1m2f win) and holds solid claims under a 6lb penalty returned to this course.

RaceName: Find Us At fitzdares.com Handicap
Spotlight: Raced only four times, winning 1m AW event then failing to progress over 1m2f on turf; beaten long way last time and needs to derive major benefit from this drop back in trip, combined with first-time headgear.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Third to a smart stablemate over 1m at Limerick (Highwayman well in arrears) and strong at the finish when second over 9.5f at Ballinrobe (third won handicap off 72 since); bred to stay and will take beating.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Second foal of unraced half-sister to winners Primo Lady (5f 2yo Listed), Zhiggy's Stardust (6f) and Lucky Mellor (5f 2yo), out of 6f 2yo winner; certainly not bred for this trip.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: The fact that he was 250-1 for his latest run over 1m at Naas tells you how little he had accomplished prior to that; came from the rear and was going on well in the final furlong when beaten 4.5l; mark looks in the right ballpark on that evidence and half-brother won over this trip in France.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Third to Causeway (unbeaten in four starts since, including a Group 2 at Royal Ascot) in 7f Curragh maiden last year; hasn't built on that; mark was put back up 5lb after fair run in valuable event at Gowran last month; all his form is with an ease and scratched because of fast ground last time; steps up in trip by two furlongs.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Sands Of Mali brother to 7.3f winner Sofian, half-brother to four other winners at distances from 7f to 1m6f; dam placed 5f-1m as 2yo.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Third to a pair of mid-80s rivals over 1m2f at Navan, beaten 5l having travelled well; looked a non-stayer over 1m4f last time; headgear.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: U S Navy Flag half-brother to five winners including Preamble (6f 2yo; RPR 88), Sonoran (7f; 85) and Never Rains (1m; 67); family of Goldikova; nice page but best watched.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Superbly bred (sibling of three Group 1 winners); 14s for 1m4f maiden at Dundalk, green and off the bridle a long way out, beaten 11l by winner who was placed in a Royal Ascot handicap next time; blinkers added.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: Five-time AW winner including over C&D; his last success was 17 months ago but he returned after a short break with an eye-catching fourth in a Wolverhampton handicap (6f, Tapeta) last week; didn't get much luck in that race and he's in the mix back up in trip; visor reapplied.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: Off the mark at the 13th attempt when landing a Bath handicap (1m, good) last month; that was a surprise at 33-1 but she responded well to new equipment and hit a personal best with that comfortable success; big player again if she can back that up.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: 15-race maiden who has been out of sorts over hurdles and on the Flat this year; hasn't been with Tony Carroll for long but he was beaten 8l in a classified event at Brighton (1m2f) last week and others are preferred; stablemate of Masqool.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: 11-time AW winner including four at this track (7f/1m2f); has a patchy record this year but he came from the rear to finish runner-up in a classified event at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last Tuesday; should get a decent tow into this race and he's respected back on Polytrack.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: Just three wins from 64 starts but he returned after a short break with a near-miss in a classified at Brighton (7f, good) last month; has possibilities back up in trip and his first two wins came on AW at Lingfield.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: Ten-race maiden who has yet to be placed and he was tailed off in an Epsom handicap (1m2f, good) 12 days ago; visor is now added and he needs a major revival back in trip.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: Won a 7f handicap here (AW) for Michael Attwater last summer but that is his sole win from 26 starts and he's generally struggled since; comes with risks attached after 165 days off but he needs checking in market for new yard.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: 3yo filly who posted a better effort with cheekpieces added when runner-up in a C&D handicap last Monday; was beaten around 4l in that race but she's still unexposed and has claims if she can build on that.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: Struggled in maiden/novice events including two tailed-off efforts at massive prices this summer; now drops into a 0-50 event but she needs a transformation at this new trip.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Low-mileage filly whose solid record features two wins over 1m, including at Windsor; finished nicely for third at Newmarket on seasonal debut and looks ready for a crack at tonight's longer distance; remains open to further progress; highly respected.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Record is only 1-19 but the success, at Nottingham, is among his recent form and puts him 1-1 under Oisin Murphy; ran creditably a few days later and remains a consistent sort; rested over five weeks since that last run; solid claims.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Commenced handicap career with a five-timer but a record of 0-4 since reflects that her ceiling has likely been reached; sole effort this term (folded tamely, well beaten) leaves a question mark over her current form.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing I and Partner
Spotlight: Has gained both wins on AW but that was the case with stablemate Cristo (declared 7:50) until he recently prevailed on turf; has posted inconsistent results since returned to this sphere, however, and isn't a solid option.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Prevailed narrowly at Newbury last time, taking record to 5-28 and confirming that he's well suited by fast ground; back up 3lb but remains well handicapped on peak form and is a dual C&D winner; good third in this race last year; one of the main players.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: P Afia, E Hobson, A Pegley, C&r Russell
Spotlight: Creditable third in both starts since making a successful reappearance; remains in form but is getting no respite from the assessor, as another 1lb rise reflects; has done all her winning off marks in the 60s.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Successful for two previous yards but holds weak claims on his efforts for current stable; campaigned mostly at about 1m and has enough to prove back up in trip; blinkers replace the cheekpieces worn last time.

RaceName: Heights Hotel, Killarney Handicap
Spotlight: Fourth in a Gowran maiden and back to form on second handicap start when staying on to be runner-up to subsequent winner Rodeo Drive at Ballinrobe (9.5f); stayers on dam's side of pedigree, but her best runs have been on easy ground.

RaceName: Heights Hotel, Killarney Handicap
Spotlight: Third to Wipeawayyourtears over 1m4f at Fairyhouse where he battled on well; 7-2 fav for second handicap start over same trip at Bellewstown, faded out of it in the final furlong; cheekpieces swapped for blinkers.

RaceName: Heights Hotel, Killarney Handicap
Spotlight: Placed on easy ground in first two handicaps and then handled good ground when winning well over 1m4f at Fairyhouse; Cleary's claim means she's effectively off the same mark.

RaceName: Heights Hotel, Killarney Handicap
OwnerName: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit
Spotlight: Handicapped on AW maiden runs; only beaten 2l over C&D on handicap debut in May; that form is only ordinary, but headgear might help.

RaceName: Heights Hotel, Killarney Handicap
Spotlight: Going the right way in handicaps; second over 1m4f at Limerick and looked in need of farther when third to good yardstick Rodeo Blues over this trip at Naas; the long straight here will suit him as he needs time to hit full stride.

RaceName: Heights Hotel, Killarney Handicap
Spotlight: Didn't have the best of runs up the straight when keeping on to fair effect at Fairyhouse (1m4f), but still has fair bit to find with both the winner Wipeawayyourtears and third Sir Trancealot.

RaceName: Heights Hotel, Killarney Handicap
Spotlight: Creditable sixth in Gowran maiden, but dismal run when 9-1 for handicap debut over 9.5f at Ballinrobe; some middle-distance performers in family.

RaceName: Heights Hotel, Killarney Handicap
Spotlight: Quiet in maidens; half-sister to a pair of winners over 1mf/1m3f; yard has had a couple run well lately.

RaceName: Heights Hotel, Killarney Handicap
Spotlight: Only 3-1 for handicap debut at Sligo in May; ran poorly but maybe soft ground was to blame; gelded since and change of headgear; with the stable so powerful in the 3yo category, plenty will afford him another chance.

RaceName: Heights Hotel, Killarney Handicap
Spotlight: Finished a head in front of Sir Trancealot when fifth against older horses at Bellewstown (1m4f); that was her best effort in handicaps and might be capable of better.

RaceName: Heights Hotel, Killarney Handicap
Spotlight: Big prices all starts; ran okay over 5f on handicap debut, but well beaten since and going half-a-mile farther today; not bred for this trip either.

RaceName: Heights Hotel, Killarney Handicap
Spotlight: Couple of creditable handicap runs over 7f/1m, placed over latter trip behind easy winner at Bellewstown; big step up in trip; dam won over almost this far in France.

RaceName: Heights Hotel, Killarney Handicap
Spotlight: Placed over 9.5f at Gowran; hard to see him reversing Naas 1m3f form with Point Cartwright.

RaceName: Heights Hotel, Killarney Handicap
Spotlight: Ran well in a nursery and fair runs behind the same horse, Rodeo Drive, in last two outings; seems safely held by Point Cartwright on latest outing at Naas.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Regressive 6yo who has not run on the Flat since midfield finish in 1m6f Wolverhampton handicap in April; ran okay in two handicap hurdles last month but others have more pressing claims.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
